title: Standing in the Hallway of the Dream House
subtitle: A journal practice for examining what you believe the right home would solve
excerpt: A journal practice for examining what you believe the dream house would actually fix. Examines the emotional freight the future home carries and what it would mean to put some of that down now.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the longing for a different home feels disproportionate, or when you suspect the dream house has become a stand-in for something else.

content.text: A journal practice for examining the dream house. The home we want often carries more than a preference for different rooms. It carries a feeling: settled, arrived, finally sorted, free to start. This practice separates what the house itself offers from what we have loaded onto it.
Describe the dream home in as much detail as you can. What does it have? What does it feel like?
Rooms, light, space, garden, kitchen, location. Write the whole thing. Do not hold back.
Now write: 'When I have this home, I will...' and finish the sentence as many times as you can.
Notice what you are holding in reserve for that home. The entertaining, the decorating, the feeling of being settled, the starting of something.
Which items on that list are actually about the house, and which are available now?
Be specific. Some things do require a different space. Others are being held back by the wrong reason.
What is one thing from that list you could do in this home this month?
Not a solution to everything. One thing. Write it down and write what is stopping you doing it here rather than there.
